title: Today's AI Brief: Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
summary: Anthropic's Code with Claude event showcased AI coding's future, while OpenAI launches a multi-year AI partnership in Singapore.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
imageCaption: Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
imageSourceTitle: Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've been following some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence, and I'm excited to share them with you.

First up, Anthropic recently hosted a two-day event for software developers in London called Code with Claude. The timing was quite a coincidence, as it kicked off on the same day as Google's I/O in Palo Alto. What caught my attention was the showcase of AI-powered coding, which gave us a glimpse into the future of software development. It seems like AI is getting more comfortable writing code, whether we like it or not.

Next, OpenAI has launched a multi-year AI partnership in Singapore. This partnership aims to expand AI deployment, build local talent, and support businesses and public services with AI. It's an interesting move, as it highlights the growing importance of AI in the region. I'm curious to see how this partnership will play out and what kind of impact it will have on the local economy.

Anthropic's event and OpenAI's partnership in Singapore are just a couple of examples of how AI is rapidly evolving. As I look at these developments, I'm struck by the pace of progress in AI. It's clear that AI is becoming increasingly integrated into various aspects of our lives, from software development to public services.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
